sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient presented with a history of right knee pain that started a week ago after an injury at a trampoline park.
He described hearing a snap and feeling instant pain, with the pain located on the outside of the knee.
He has not taken any pain medication other than Tylenol, which provided minimal relief.
He has been elevating the knee and has not used ice or heat.
He has a history of hypertension and diabetes, for which he is currently taking lisinopril and metformin.
Past medical history: The patient has hypertension and diabetes.
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications: Lisinopril 20mg daily for hypertension, metformin 500mg daily for diabetes.
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure 180/80, pulse rate not mentioned, oxygen saturation not mentioned, temperature not mentioned.
Physical exam: The patient had a regular heart rate and rhythm, clear lungs bilaterally, and no murmur.
The knee examination revealed ecchymosis and edema on the lateral aspect, but no effusion.
The patient had a slight limp and could bend and straighten the knee, but with some pain.
The patient had a slight gait disturbance.
Test Results: Right knee x-ray showed no evidence of fracture or bony abnormality.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a lateral ligament strain of the right knee and hypertension, and is at risk for uncontrolled diabetes.
Problem list: Right knee pain; hypertension; uncontrolled diabetes; suspected hyperlipidemia.
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam 15mg daily for pain and swelling of the right knee.
I referred the patient to physical therapy to strengthen the muscles around the knee and prevent further injury.
I will continue the lisinopril at 20mg daily and order an echocardiogram to assess cardiac function.
I will also order an A1c and lipid panel to assess long-term blood sugar and lipid levels.
Follow-up: The patient will return for follow-up after the physical therapy and echocardiogram results are available, and to discuss further management of hypertension and diabetes.
